space_library.name: Pre-Function Lobby
notes: GSA defines a pre-function lobby as the room used for entry from the outside in a performing arts building where the audience gathers before showtime and at intermission.

space_library.name: Ticketing / Box Office
notes: Ticketbooths/POS stations need ADA-accessible point-of-sale and flexible queue planning in high-volume conditions.

space_library.name: Performance Hall / Audience Chamber
notes: GSA defines performance hall and audience seating spaces as core audience spaces; acoustic and visual performance are critical.

space_library.name: Acting Stage / Stage House
notes: GSA defines acting stage, stage wings, catwalks, orchestra pit, and supporting performance spaces as core theater components.

space_library.name: Backstage Support / Dressing
notes: Supporting performance spaces include dressing, green room, wardrobe, and artist support.

space_library.name: Rehearsal Room
notes: GSA defines performance rehearsal space for preparatory music and theater performances before public performance.

space_library.name: Control / Projection Booth
notes: Smithsonian guidance places remote lighting controls in the projection booth/control console and requires specialist lighting and acoustics input for theaters.

space_library.name: Public Restrooms
notes: Distributed to support pre-function and intermission surges.

space_library.name: Loading Dock / Scene Shop
notes: Backstage support works best with direct truck/service access for scenery, instruments, and equipment.

envelope_assembly.wall: high-mass acoustic envelope with insulated backup wall
envelope_assembly.glazing: limited lobby glazing with controlled light spill into hall

plan_archetypes.notes: Public front-of-house and controlled backstage/service zones should remain distinct.
